title: MCMC Scam Alert: Fraudulent SMS Claiming EPF/KWSP Withdrawal Approval
content: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has identified a widespread SMS phishing campaign targeting Malaysians with fraudulent messages claiming their Employees Provident Fund (KWSP/EPF) withdrawal has been approved. The SMS messages typically read: 'KWSP: Pengeluaran akaun anda sebanyak RM 10,000 telah diluluskan. Klik pautan untuk pengesahan: [malicious link]' (Translation: 'EPF: Your account withdrawal of RM 10,000 has been approved. Click the link for verification'). The link directs victims to a convincing replica of the KWSP i-Akaun login page hosted on domains such as kwsp-iakaunmy.com, epf-malaysia-verify.com, and similar typosquatted domains. Once victims enter their credentials, the scammers gain access to their EPF accounts and initiate unauthorised withdrawals via compromised bank accounts. Between September and November 2025, MCMC blocked over 2,800 phishing domains related to this campaign and reported 14,600 fraudulent SMS numbers to telecommunications providers for blocking. MCMC reminds the public: EPF/KWSP will NEVER send SMS messages with clickable links. Always access your EPF account only through the official website (www.kwsp.gov.my) or the official i-Akaun app. Report suspicious SMS to MCMC via the portal at https://aduan.skmm.gov.my or call 1-800-188-030.
